summary: Frontend-only verification of the D2Synergy Armor Mods (Armor 3.0) rewrite. Loaded the static app at the preview URL, expanded panel '05 Armor Mods', enumerated every dropdown option in all 5 slots (Helmet/Arms/Chest/Legs/Class Item), cross-checked against the requested spec, verified all 10 obsolete mods are gone, exercised a live selection to confirm the description line + per-slot 'Mod energy: X / 10' readout and over-capacity warning behavior. Zero console errors before, during, or after interactions.
critical_code_review_comments: File /app/frontend/public/index.html is 4473 lines (single-file vanilla app). Well above the 700-line guideline; consider splitting DATA (ARMOR_MODS_BY_SLOT, TUNING_MODS_BY_SLOT, ARMOR_SETS, ARTIFACT_PERK_LIBRARY, WEAPONS, etc.) into separate <script src> modules or a data.js file to improve maintainability. This is a code-organization concern only — functionality is correct.

The state schema allows the same mod to be picked in multiple slot indices of the same armor piece (verified by successfully setting Helmet Slot 1..4 all to 'Dynamo'). In real Destiny 2 you can stack identical mods across slots, so this may be intended, but there is no UI hint of the stacking rule; consider a small note near the panel header if unstacked-per-slot behavior is intended in the future.
Dropdown option text uses raw '⚡' + ' (utility)' suffix concatenation; consider a small helper to render this consistently (very minor).
test_credentials: N/A — client-side static app, no auth required.
context_for_next_testing_agent: App is a single-file vanilla HTML/CSS/JS Destiny 2 build-crafting tool at /app/frontend/public/index.html (no backend, no React). Preview URL: https://synergy-builder-1.preview.emergentagent.com . Armor Mods panel is '05 Armor Mods' in the left builder column; slot wraps are divs containing exactly 4 '.slotrow' selects (Slot 1..4). Slot label appears as first-child div (uppercase text like 'HELMET'). Tuning select follows the mod-energy row. To interact with selects programmatically without hunting selectors, use page.evaluate with a filter for divs containing 4 '.slotrow' selects and set select.value + dispatch a 'change' Event. The 'Mod energy: X / 10' readout switches to var(--danger) color and appends ' — over capacity' when > 10.

verifications.mod_selection_shows_energy_desc: Selecting Helmet Slot 1 = 'Ashes to Assets' produced description: 'Super Energy Gain +6%  ·  1⚡ energy'
verifications.per_slot_energy_total: Helmet readout became 'Mod energy: 1 / 10' (muted color) after 1 mod.
verifications.over_capacity_behavior: Filling Helmet with 4x Dynamo produced 'Mod energy: 12 / 10 — over capacity' rendered in var(--danger) color.
